title: Ron And Diane

jokes.text: Merry Congratu-Christmas!
jokes.setup: Leslie needs to congratulate Ron while also saying Merry Christmas
jokes.punchline: She creates a nonsensical portmanteau word
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie over-enthusiasm creating absurd word combinations. Good character work and economy.

jokes.text: I've had a 'Ron Swanson' Google alert for seven years and it finally paid off
jokes.setup: Leslie knows about Ron's award nomination
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's been obsessively monitoring him online for years
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment - her obsessive friendship taken to absurd but believable lengths.

jokes.text: Recently, I made a chair. When I was finished, I thought it was a good chair.
jokes.setup: Ron describing his award-nominated woodworking
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement delivered completely deadpan
jokes.explanation: Pure Ron Swanson - taking something significant and describing it with minimal emotion.

jokes.text: It's been a real whirlwind
jokes.setup: Ron describing the excitement of his nomination process
jokes.punchline: Describing the most mundane process as a 'whirlwind'
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery but fairly predictable Ron humor.

jokes.text: This might be the first time I've ever wanted to attend an event
jokes.setup: Ron expressing enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's never wanted to attend any event before
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing Ron's antisocial nature.

jokes.text: As your self-appointed emotional guardian, it is my duty to love and support you
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining why she's going to the event
jokes.punchline: She's appointed herself as Ron's emotional guardian
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Leslie's boundary-crossing friendship style.

jokes.text: ♪ Jingle bells, jingle yay, jingle good for you ♪
jokes.setup: Leslie threatens to sing a congratulatory Christmas carol
jokes.punchline: The actual lyrics are nonsensical and awkward
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie over-enthusiasm creating cringe-worthy but endearing moments.

jokes.text: I'm allergic to sushi. Every time I eat more than 80 sushis, I barf
jokes.setup: Tom claims to be allergic to sushi
jokes.punchline: The 'allergy' only kicks in after an impossibly large amount
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic that reveals Tom's gluttony while claiming victimhood.

jokes.text: ♪ Jerry Dinner ♪ Jerry Dinner
jokes.setup: They need to pick a restaurant
jokes.punchline: They have a whole song for their cruel Jerry tradition
jokes.explanation: Sets up the Jerry Dinner concept with characteristic cruelty sung cheerfully.

jokes.text: Every time we laugh at something stupid Jerry does, we put a dollar in this box
jokes.setup: Explaining Jerry Dinner
jokes.punchline: They profit from mocking their coworker
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that establishes the cruel but systematic nature of their Jerry mockery.

jokes.text: $516... Aw, jeez! And that's 517
jokes.setup: Tom reveals how much money they've collected from Jerry's mistakes
jokes.punchline: Jerry's reaction to learning about it immediately adds another dollar
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback - Jerry's reaction to the cruelty becomes more fuel for it.

jokes.setup: Jerry continues reacting
jokes.punchline: The relentless counting continues
jokes.explanation: Good follow-through on the escalation, though less impact than the initial reveal.

jokes.text: It's not mean if he doesn't know about it. It's like talking about people behind their backs. Everybody wins
jokes.setup: Ann says the Jerry Dinner is mean
jokes.punchline: April's twisted logic that ignorance makes cruelty acceptable
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing April's amoral worldview presented as logic.

jokes.text: That's hilarious. You should do stand-up. If you're kidding, you suck, but if you're serious, I actually have been thinking about it
jokes.setup: Tom mocking Ann's suggestion to invite Jerry
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's actually considering stand-up comedy
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection showing Tom's delusions about his comedic talents.

jokes.text: Hey, April, Matlock called. He wants his cardigan back. Boom! I'm out
jokes.setup: Tom trying out his comedy material
jokes.punchline: Generic old-person joke delivered with unwarranted confidence
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad joke that works because it shows Tom's lack of comedy talent.

jokes.text: Who's Matlock?
jokes.setup: Tom's dated reference
jokes.punchline: April's genuine confusion destroys his joke
jokes.explanation: Perfect puncture of Tom's confidence with generational reality check.

jokes.text: This time, the giant spider got caught in my web. Progress
jokes.setup: Andy talking about his dream journal
jokes.punchline: He sees becoming the predator in nightmares as therapeutic progress
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment showing his childlike logic about confronting fears.

jokes.text: You have Leslie and I have my therapist, Dr. Richard Nygard, who I see 15 times a week
jokes.setup: Andy explaining why they don't hang out much
jokes.punchline: Reveals impossibly frequent therapy schedule
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Andy's commitment to self-improvement taken to absurd lengths.

jokes.text: Ron has told me so much about you, in that he has told me your name is Diane, and you exist
jokes.setup: Leslie doing the polite 'he's told me so much about you'
jokes.punchline: Reveals Ron has shared almost no information
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure and character work - perfectly captures Ron's secretiveness and Leslie's honesty.

jokes.text: Oh, it's Ulysses. I can see why he didn't tell me that
jokes.setup: Diane doesn't know Ron's middle name
jokes.punchline: Leslie reveals it's the embarrassing 'Ulysses'
jokes.explanation: Good reveal that adds character depth while being embarrassing for Ron.

jokes.text: Mary, mother of God. That's Christian Becksvoort!
jokes.setup: Leslie spots someone at the woodworking event
jokes.punchline: She's as excited about a woodworker as others would be about a celebrity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie's enthusiasm for niche expertise.

jokes.text: What are your five biggest hopes, dreams, fears, and regrets?
jokes.setup: Leslie wants to get to know Diane
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks for impossibly personal information
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie boundary-crossing disguised as friendly interest.

jokes.text: The time he slipped on a cinnabon and landed in a bunch of garbage
jokes.setup: Sharing favorite Jerry moments
jokes.punchline: Describes a humiliating physical comedy scenario
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy scenario, elevated slightly by April's deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: My favorite Jerry moment was when he ate a bowl of glue
jokes.setup: Another Jerry story
jokes.punchline: Jerry literally ate glue
jokes.explanation: Absurdist escalation that sets up the next joke well.

jokes.text: My favorite Jerry moment is when you switched out his potato soup with glue and he didn't know
jokes.setup: Tom's glue story
jokes.punchline: Reveals Tom deliberately fed Jerry glue
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes Tom's story even darker by revealing deliberate cruelty.

jokes.text: I forgot to put on deodorant. And a jacket and one of my socks. Plus, I gotta poop, but I could stop anywhere for that
jokes.setup: Andy needs to stop at home
jokes.punchline: Increasingly inappropriate oversharing ending with bathroom needs
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy oversharing with good escalation to increasingly inappropriate details.

jokes.text: Smart, funny, independent, and sexy? Diane Lewis? More like Diane Sawyer
jokes.setup: Leslie describing Diane's positive qualities
jokes.punchline: Compares her to news anchor with similar name
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay that relies on name similarity without much cleverness.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Jerry's having a Christmas party! I can't believe he didn't invite us. How dare he! What'd we ever do to him?
jokes.setup: They see Jerry's Christmas party
jokes.punchline: Tom acts outraged while being completely oblivious to their cruelty
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - Tom's complete lack of self-awareness is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Remain calm, but I think you just drank some of the regular, full-fat eggnog
jokes.setup: Chris enjoying eggnog
jokes.punchline: Ben reveals it contains Chris's forbidden fat
jokes.explanation: Sets up Chris's health obsession for the following reaction.

jokes.text: No, but it will add exactly 440 calories. But you know what? I'm fine. You only live once
jokes.setup: Chris learns he drank full-fat eggnog
jokes.punchline: Immediately calculates exact calories but tries to be philosophical
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chris's internal struggle between health obsession and trying to be normal.

jokes.text: I don't know if you know this, but things with fat in them taste way better than things with no fat. Yeah, Chris-- everybody knows that
jokes.setup: Chris discovering the taste benefits of fat
jokes.punchline: Ben's deadpan response to Chris stating the obvious
jokes.explanation: Good straight man response to Chris's naive food discovery.

jokes.text: She is at the bar right now ordering a Lagavulin, neat for you. I mean, she's even putting up with all this stupid, boring woodworking stuff. I'm sorry, but, you know, it's not the Super Bowl, guys
jokes.setup: Leslie praising Diane
jokes.punchline: Accidentally insults Ron's passion in front of woodworking enthusiasts
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Leslie's enthusiasm leading to social blunders.

jokes.text: Hello, you gorgeous craftsmen. Wow, look at this room. So much wood, ready to be worked
jokes.setup: Tammy enters the woodworking event
jokes.punchline: Turns innocent woodworking language into sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual innuendo that fits Tammy's predatory character.

jokes.text: Tammy, this is Diane. Diane, this is a piece of human garbage named Tammy, who is also my ex-wife. Twice ex-wife. We were married twice. And divorced twice
jokes.setup: Ron introducing Tammy to Diane
jokes.punchline: Increasingly hostile introduction revealing their complex history
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Ron's uncharacteristic emotional openness about Tammy.

jokes.text: Oh, I just have a little something I need to get drilled
jokes.setup: Explaining why she's at a woodworking event
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo disguised as innocent woodworking need
jokes.explanation: Predictable sexual innuendo that works because it's so obviously Tammy.

jokes.text: Shouldn't you be at the library, forcing people to borrow books? Leslie, you and I both know that the library closes at 3:00 p.m. Of course it does
jokes.setup: Leslie questioning why Tammy isn't at work
jokes.punchline: Reveals the library has absurdly early hours
jokes.explanation: Good dig at inefficient government services through library hours.

jokes.text: This may be the hardest challenge yet for Leslie Knope, emotional guardian. I need to protect a sweet couple from a sex-crazed demon librarian who makes me question my stance on using the 'B' word
jokes.setup: Leslie in confessional about the Tammy situation
jokes.punchline: Tammy is so awful she makes Leslie consider using profanity
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing how Tammy challenges even Leslie's moral standards.

jokes.text: What? I don't see any gingerbread cookies. I'm talking about that jacked-up, light-skinned dude. Look like Blake Griffin
jokes.setup: Donna talking about a gingerbread man
jokes.punchline: She's describing a muscular person, not a cookie
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that's enhanced by Donna's characteristic appreciation for attractive men.

jokes.text: There's a buffet-- in the house! I didn't even know you could do that!
jokes.setup: Andy seeing Jerry's party setup
jokes.punchline: Acts like having food inside a house is revolutionary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment showing his childlike wonder at basic concepts.

jokes.text: I need a place to deliver this baby, Ann. It's coming out of me right now. I'm pregnant
jokes.setup: April trying to get into Jerry's party
jokes.punchline: Completely fabricated emergency that makes no physical sense
jokes.explanation: Good April moment showing her willingness to say anything ridiculous to get her way.

jokes.text: You're so weak, really? I mean, I'm barely even doing anything. Are you iron-deficient? Let me look at your palm
jokes.setup: April and Ann struggling
jokes.punchline: April casually diagnoses Ann while overpowering her
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing April's unexpected knowledge and casual cruelty.

jokes.text: It's because of your man strength, Man Perkins
jokes.setup: Tom explaining why April is stronger
jokes.punchline: Gives Ann a masculine nickname based on her surname
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay that fits Tom's pattern of giving people nicknames.

jokes.text: Sorry, guys. This is your penance. You can come in if you do something nice for Jerry.
jokes.setup: Tom and April wanting to enter the party
jokes.punchline: Ann using their exclusion as moral instruction
jokes.explanation: Good Ann moment showing her moral stance. The word 'penance' elevates it beyond simple punishment.

jokes.text: It's cold outside, and I can't wear mittens 'cause they're unflattering to my hands
jokes.setup: Tom pleading to get into the party
jokes.punchline: Cares more about hand aesthetics than warmth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom vanity moment showing his misplaced priorities.

jokes.text: Merry critches, bitches!
jokes.setup: Donna getting into the party while others are left out
jokes.punchline: Celebrates with portmanteau of Christmas greetings and insult
jokes.explanation: Good Donna moment with efficient wordplay and attitude.

jokes.text: I'm your host, Martin Housely, and wow, it's gonna be a great show-- knock on wood
jokes.setup: Host introducing woodworking awards
jokes.punchline: Uses wood-related idiom at wood event
jokes.explanation: Weak, obvious pun that any host would make. Predictable dad joke level.

jokes.text: A guy traded me his seat for a peek and a squeeze. That's my boob and my butt, respectively
jokes.setup: Tammy explaining how she got her seat
jokes.punchline: Casually describes sexual transaction with anatomical clarification
jokes.explanation: Tammy's characteristic inappropriate oversharing with unnecessary clarification.

jokes.text: Well, you know, they asked me if I would host this event, and I said, 'Wood I?'
jokes.setup: Host explaining how he got the job
jokes.punchline: Another obvious wood pun
jokes.explanation: Another weak, obvious pun. The host character serves as example of bad comedy.

jokes.text: Gayle-- you're Gayle? Jerry's beautiful wife... Who looks like that
jokes.setup: Chris meeting Jerry's wife
jokes.punchline: His shock that Jerry landed someone so attractive
jokes.explanation: Sets up the ongoing Jerry paradox mystery - how he got such an attractive wife.

jokes.text: Well, look who's a silver fox
jokes.setup: Gayle removing something from Chris's shoulder
jokes.punchline: She finds him attractive, adding to the Jerry mystery
jokes.explanation: Adds to the Gayle mystery by showing her attraction to conventionally handsome men.

jokes.text: My, my. Somebody's being a gloomy goose. Yeah. Relax, gloomy goose
jokes.setup: Chris expressing mortality concerns
jokes.punchline: The Gergiches use cutesy language for serious topics
jokes.explanation: Good family characterization showing their relentless positivity.

jokes.text: ♪ Here we go a-caroling, among the leaves so green... ♪
jokes.setup: The Gergich family performs for their guests
jokes.punchline: Elaborate family musical performance at casual party
jokes.explanation: Visual gag showing the Gergich family's earnest over-enthusiasm for holidays.

jokes.text: Jerry Filter! Who's Jerry Filter? Unsurprisingly, Jerry sends a lot of annoying emails. So a while ago, I put a filter on all our accounts. Everything from Jerry goes directly to spam
jokes.setup: They wonder why they didn't get party invites
jokes.punchline: April has been auto-deleting all of Jerry's emails for years
jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal that recontextualizes their confusion and shows April's systematic cruelty.

jokes.text: Congratulations on your wedding. I'm rooting for you kids. Jerry. Hope you have the best birthday, Tom. I just tied my high score in Snake
jokes.setup: Reading Jerry's filtered emails
jokes.punchline: Jerry's messages are genuinely kind, ending with innocent game achievement
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing Jerry's genuine kindness contrasted with their cruelty to him.

jokes.text: This award is the only one I'd ever give a damn about
jokes.setup: Ron wins the woodworking award
jokes.punchline: Reveals genuine emotion through careful word choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment showing rare genuine emotion through his characteristic restraint.

jokes.text: I made my first chair when I was five, but the quality of the wood was wanting, so when I turned nine, I used my factory wages to purchase some beautiful local walnut
jokes.setup: Ron giving acceptance speech
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions having factory wages at age nine
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment casually revealing Ron's impossible childhood work history.

jokes.text: Uh, thank you... for this... Uh, this all good-- night good. Uh... There it is
jokes.setup: Ron trying to finish his speech
jokes.punchline: Completely breaks down verbally despite his usual confidence
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ron flustered by Tammy's presence despite his usual composure.

jokes.text: Man, maybe going to therapy 1,000 times a week is helping. I mean, if you'd seen that picture a month ago, you would have fallen apart. It's just a picture
jokes.setup: Andy seems fine looking at photo of his ex
jokes.punchline: Ben exaggerates Andy's therapy frequency, then Andy immediately contradicts the progress
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Andy's excessive therapy with misdirection about his emotional progress.

jokes.text: You're the one who's been at his side all night. You're the one who's been protecting him, you're the one who knows all the intimate details of his romantic history with that monster... so if there is a threat to my relationship with Ron, it's not Tammy. It's you
jokes.setup: Diane seemingly understanding about Tammy
jokes.punchline: Reveals she sees Leslie as the real threat
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and character insight that reframes the entire conflict.

jokes.text: You're pro-government, you never stop talking, and you have blonde hair. You're my worst nightmare
jokes.setup: Ron dismissing romantic possibilities with Leslie
jokes.punchline: Lists specific reasons why she's incompatible, ending with hair color
jokes.explanation: Good character moment listing Ron's specific anti-government, anti-social values.

jokes.text: Excuse me, one man's 'worst nightmare' is every other man's 'total package'
jokes.setup: Ron calling Leslie his worst nightmare
jokes.punchline: Leslie's confident assertion of her universal appeal
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie moment showing her confidence and ability to reframe insults as compliments.

jokes.text: I know that you secretly love artichokes and plums. Keep your voice down, woman!
jokes.setup: Leslie proving how well she knows Ron
jokes.punchline: Ron panics about his food preferences being revealed
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ron's food shame and Leslie's intimate knowledge of his secrets.

jokes.text: He was the young rafting instructor all the girls were into, and I was the young rafting instructor all the guys were into... And then we got together. Ah, so random, right?
jokes.setup: Explaining how she met her fiancé
jokes.punchline: Both were attractive rafting instructors, calling it 'random' when it's perfectly logical
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing attractive people's obliviousness to their advantages.

jokes.text: If therapy has taught me anything, it's that I need to face my fears, even when my fears are engaged to a man with the chiseled jawline of a young me
jokes.setup: Chris reflecting on seeing his ex
jokes.punchline: Describes the fiancé's attractiveness by comparing to himself
jokes.explanation: Good Chris moment showing his narcissism even when being self-aware.

jokes.text: So Gayle and Jerry-- I've thought about it a lot. There's no logical explanation
jokes.setup: Ben contemplating the Jerry marriage mystery
jokes.punchline: Admits defeat in understanding how Jerry got Gayle
jokes.explanation: Good callback acknowledging the ongoing Jerry paradox that mystifies everyone.

jokes.text: I thought you weren't coming. I--you know, never got your RSVP... we took up a collection to pay your hospital bills, since you almost farted yourself to death
jokes.setup: Jerry being gracious about them not RSVPing
jokes.punchline: Tom's 'gift' references a humiliating Jerry incident
jokes.explanation: Shows the contrast between Jerry's graciousness and their continued mockery.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, I am so turned on right now. What is wrong with you?
jokes.setup: Leslie fighting Tammy over car keys
jokes.punchline: Tammy finds the physical confrontation sexually exciting
jokes.explanation: Characteristic Tammy moment sexualizing inappropriate situations.

jokes.text: I would sooner visit Europe than have something romantic happen between us. Although, if you'd like to visit Europe, I like you so much, I'd be willing to risk it. But not France, right? God, no.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining his lack of romantic interest in Leslie
jokes.punchline: His hatred of Europe, willingness to compromise, and specific hatred of France
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment combining his isolationism, romantic dedication, and specific prejudices. Great escalation structure.

jokes.text: I would sooner visit Europe than have something romantic happen between us. Although, if you'd like to visit Europe, I like you so much, I'd be willing to risk it. But not France, right? God, no
jokes.setup: Ron explaining his lack of romantic interest in Leslie
jokes.punchline: Shows he'd overcome his greatest fear for Diane, except for France
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ron moment showing romantic growth while maintaining his core character traits.

jokes.text: A mighty fine holiday evening to you all. It might be cold outside, but it's about to get warm all up in my jazz
jokes.setup: Ron revealing his secret identity as jazz musician
jokes.punchline: Adopts completely different persona with smooth jazz talk
jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal showing Ron's hidden artistic side with perfect character contrast.

jokes.text: I, of course, am Duke Silver, and I would like to dedicate this first song... To my duchess.
jokes.setup: Ron's secret identity as a jazz musician
jokes.punchline: His smooth persona and romantic dedication
jokes.explanation: Major character reveal that pays off the Duke Silver mystery. The contrast with regular Ron is perfect.

jokes.text: Hey, Ron, why don't you get me out of these handcuffs, so I can put you into these handcuffs. Yes, please officer, get her out of those cuffs. I want this to be a fair fight
jokes.setup: Tammy in police custody making suggestive comments
jokes.punchline: Leslie wants to fight Tammy one-on-one without advantages
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Leslie's determination to protect Ron and defeat Tammy properly.

jokes.text: Help
jokes.setup: The ongoing Leslie vs. Tammy fight
jokes.punchline: Leslie's simple plea for help after extended battle
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective ending showing Leslie's defeat with maximum economy.

tmdb_overview: Ron, Diane, and Leslie attend a wood working awards dinner after one of Ron's handcrafted chairs is nominated, but the happy occasion is threatened by the arrival of Tammy II. Meanwhile, Jerry hosts a holiday party at his home with his wife, and some of his friends are shocked to find they were not invited.
insight: Ron's wedding episode trades punchline density for character grounding, landing 73 jokes at subdued 2.99 per minute.
